findings.title: Nominal reserve accounting breaks with fee-on-transfer, deflationary, or rebasing tokens and can be crystallized with `gulp()`

best_agent_match.title: Nominal-amount reserve accounting lets fee-on-transfer or rebasing tokens drain LP value

findings.title: Underlying token transfers are trusted by boolean return value only, so a malicious bound token can fake deposits or payouts

best_agent_match.title: A single paused or blacklisting token can brick finalized pool exits and strand value

findings.title: Finalized pools have no recovery path if a bound token later blocks transfers, which can strand LP value

best_agent_match.title: A malicious bound token can fake deposits because transfers are trusted by return value only

rejected_candidates.title: BPT `approve` is vulnerable to the standard allowance race
rejected_candidates.reason: Standard ERC20 allowance race condition; user-side approval workflow issue rather than a Balancer-specific protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: BPT `transferFrom` emits an incorrect `Approval` event
rejected_candidates.reason: Off-chain event/accounting inconsistency only; it does not create an on-chain loss, lockup, or manipulation path.

rejected_candidates.title: Integer Overflow in bpow Approximation Function
rejected_candidates.reason: `bpowi()` uses checked `bmul()` arithmetic, so overflow reverts rather than silently corrupting pricing.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked External Call Return Value
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract does check the returned boolean, and state-changing entrypoints are guarded by `_lock_`; the non-returning-ERC20 compatibility issue is not a separate reportable exploit here.

rejected_candidates.title: Controller Can Manipulate Pool Parameters Arbitrarily
rejected_candidates.reason: Privileged controller behavior is an explicit trust assumption of the design, not a code vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Division by Zero in bdiv
rejected_candidates.reason: `bdiv()` explicitly checks `b != 0` and guards multiplication overflow; the stated silent-failure path is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient Slippage Protection in swapExactAmountIn
rejected_candidates.reason: MEV/sandwich risk is not caused by a missing contract check here; users already supply `minAmountOut` and `maxPrice` bounds.

rejected_candidates.title: Token Balance Desynchronization During Joins
rejected_candidates.reason: If `transferFrom()` returns `false`, the `require` reverts and state rolls back; only the distinct 'returns true without transferring' case survives and is captured in F-002.

rejected_candidates.title: Deprecated floating point in increaseApproval
rejected_candidates.reason: `increaseApproval()` / `decreaseApproval()` use checked math and the claim does not identify a concrete exploitable path.
